title: Buchanan v. Warley
summary: The Supreme Court struck down a Louisville residential segregation ordinance that barred Black people from buying property on majority-white blocks and white people from buying property on majority-Black blocks.
outcome_summary: Blocked one form of legally mandated residential segregation, though private covenants and other discriminatory housing practices continued.
impact_notes: Important early housing-rights decision that limited explicit racial zoning.

scores.notes: Meaningful legal check on racial zoning, but many segregation tools survived.

title: Students for Fair Admissions v. Harvard and UNC
summary: The Supreme Court held that the race-conscious admissions programs challenged at Harvard and the University of North Carolina violated constitutional and statutory equal-protection rules, sharply restricting affirmative action in higher education.
outcome_summary: Narrowed one of the main remaining legal avenues universities had used to pursue racial diversity in admissions.
impact_notes: Major contemporary rollback of affirmative-action doctrine in higher education.

scores.notes: High-impact modern ruling restricting race-conscious admissions.

sources.source_title: Students for Fair Admissions, Inc. v. President and Fellows of Harvard College / UNC
sources.publisher: Supreme Court of the United States
